original_description: Shape Up methodology: design before implementation. NO IMPLEMENTATION BEFORE ALIGNMENT. Use when: planning features, estimating work, starting a new task, architectural decisions. Trigger on: "nueva feature", "cómo abordar", "diseña", "planifica", "cómo implemento", "qué enfoque".
best_description: Shape Up methodology: design before implementation. NO IMPLEMENTATION BEFORE ALIGNMENT. Use when: planning features, estimating work, starting a new task, architectural decisions. Trigger on: "nueva feature", "cómo abordar", "diseña", "planifica", "cómo implemento", "qué enfoque".
final_description: Use when someone has a feature requirement or goal but needs to figure out the approach and scope before writing code. The user knows WHAT they want but not HOW to bound or structure the work. Signals: "how do I approach X?", "where do I start with Y?", "help me scope/plan Z", "the client/PM wants W — what do we do?", "cómo lo abordo", "por dónde empiezo", "qué enfoque tomamos", "ayúdame a acotarlo". This applies even when the query starts with "I need to implement X" — if they're asking how to tackle it rather than asking you to do it, use this skill. Skip when the path is already clear and the user wants execution: fix this bug, write this endpoint, optimize this query, configure this tool, review this PR.

history.description: Shape Up methodology: design before implementation. NO IMPLEMENTATION BEFORE ALIGNMENT. Use when: planning features, estimating work, starting a new task, architectural decisions. Trigger on: "nueva feature", "cómo abordar", "diseña", "planifica", "cómo implemento", "qué enfoque".

history.description: Invoke when the user needs to plan before building: new feature requests that need scoping, "how do I approach X?" questions, help defining scope to avoid rabbit holes, or architectural decisions before coding starts. The signal is a user with a requirement but no clear path — they need problem definition, option evaluation, and a recommendation before any implementation begins. Works for requests from PMs, clients, or the user themselves. Do NOT invoke for direct implementation tasks (write this endpoint, fix this bug, configure this tool, optimize this query) where the task is already fully defined and the user just wants execution.
